transport.h(3) Library Functions Manual transport.h(3)

NAME

transport.h - Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

SYNOPSIS

Data Structures


struct acip_transport_methods_t
Transport method table (virtual function table). struct acip_transport
Transport instance structure.

Enumerations


enum acip_transport_type_t { ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3, ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4, ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5 }
Transport type enumeration.

Functions


void acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t *transport)
Destroy transport and free all resources. acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create TCP transport from existing socket. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create (const char *name, const char *url, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket client transport. acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create (const char *name, struct lws *wsi, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)
Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct webrtc_peer_connection *peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *data_channel, crypto_context_t *crypto_ctx)

Detailed Description

Transport abstraction layer for ACIP protocol.

Provides a transport-agnostic interface that allows ACIP protocol handlers to work with any underlying transport (TCP, WebSocket, HTTP, etc.).

Transport Layer Design

Architecture

The transport layer abstracts protocol delivery, allowing the same ACIP protocol handlers to work with multiple underlying transports:

  • TCP (acip_tcp_transport_create): Raw TCP sockets, binary packet streaming
  • WebSocket (acip_websocket_client_transport_create): HTTP upgrade, frame-based
  • WebRTC (acip_webrtc_transport_create): P2P data channels, encryption at transport
  • HTTP (future): Long-polling, REST-style
  • QUIC (future): UDP with congestion control

Design Principles

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with any transport implementation
3.
Each transport handles its own connection state and reliability
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic (acip_*) and transport logic

Packet Framing

Transports handle packet framing transparently:

  • TCP: Uses packet_header_t (magic + type + length + CRC32 + client_id)
  • WebSocket: Wraps packets in WebSocket frames
  • WebRTC: Sends packets directly on data channel

Version Information

  • Transport API Version: 1.0 (stable)
  • Supported ACIP Version: 1.0
  • Release Date: January 2026

DESIGN GOALS:

1.
Protocol code never calls socket()/send()/recv() directly
2.
Same ACIP handlers work with TCP, WebSocket, or any future transport
3.
Transport implementations handle their own connection state
4.
Clean separation between protocol logic and transport logic

USAGE PATTERN: 

// Create TCP transport from existing socket
acip_transport_t *tcp = acip_tcp_transport_create(sockfd, crypto_ctx);
// Send packet with type-specific helper
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(tcp, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Receive packet with dispatcher
acip_client_receive_and_dispatch(tcp, &callbacks);
// Or create WebSocket client transport
acip_transport_t *ws = acip_websocket_client_transport_create("ws://localhost:27225", crypto_ctx);
asciichat_error_t result = acip_send_ascii_frame(ws, frame_data, frame_size, ...);
// Cleanup
acip_transport_destroy(tcp);
acip_transport_destroy(ws);

MEMORY OWNERSHIP:

  • Transport owns its connection state (socket, pointers, etc.)
  • send() does NOT take ownership of data (caller must keep it valid)
  • recv() allocates buffer, caller must free via out_allocated_buffer
  • destroy() cleans up all transport resources and frees transport structure

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

January 2026

Version

1.0 (Transport API)

Definition in file transport.h.

Enumeration Type Documentation

enum acip_transport_type_t

Transport type enumeration. Identifies which transport implementation is being used. Useful for debugging and conditional logic.

Enumerator

ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP
Raw TCP socket.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET
WebSocket over TCP.
ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC
WebRTC DataChannel (P2P).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP
HTTP long-polling (future).
ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC
QUIC/UDP (future).

Definition at line 93 of file transport.h.

93              {
94   ACIP_TRANSPORT_TCP = 1,       
95   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBSOCKET = 2, 
96   ACIP_TRANSPORT_WEBRTC = 3,    
97   ACIP_TRANSPORT_HTTP = 4,      
98   ACIP_TRANSPORT_QUIC = 5       
99 } acip_transport_type_t;










Function
  Documentation




acip_transport_t * acip_tcp_transport_create (const char *
  name, socket_t sockfd, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Create TCP transport from existing socket.


Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_client_5")


sockfd Connected socket descriptor


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context (may be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Note


Caller retains socket ownership (transport doesn't close
  on destroy)


Definition at line 344 of file tcp/transport.c.


344                                                                                                              {
345   if (!name) {
346     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
347     return NULL;
348   }
349 
350   if (sockfd == INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
351     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ INVALID_SOCKET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
352     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid socket descriptor");
353     return NULL;
354   }
355 
356   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 CREATE_START: sockfd=%d, crypto=%s", sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "yes" : "no");
357 
358   // Allocate transport structure
359   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
360   if (!transport) {
361     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TRANSPORT_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(acip_transport_t));
362     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport");
363     return NULL;
364   }
365 
366   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TRANSPORT_ALLOCATED: transport=%p", (void *)transport);
367 
368   // Allocate TCP-specific data
369   tcp_transport_data_t *tcp_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t), tcp_transport_data_t *);
370   if (!tcp_data) {
371     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ ALLOC_TCP_DATA_FAILED: size=%zu", sizeof(tcp_transport_data_t));
372     SAFE_FREE(transport);
373     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate TCP transport data");
374     return NULL;
375   }
376 
377   log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_ALLOCATED: tcp_data=%p", (void *)tcp_data);
378 
379   // Initialize TCP data
380   tcp_data->sockfd = sockfd;
381   tcp_data->is_connected = true;
382 
383   // Initialize send mutex to protect concurrent sends from multiple threads
384   int mutex_result = mutex_init(&tcp_data->send_mutex, "tcp_send");
385   if (mutex_result != 0) {
386     log_error("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ❌ MUTEX_INIT_FAILED: result=%d", mutex_result);
387     SAFE_FREE(tcp_data);
388     SAFE_FREE(transport);
389     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
390     return NULL;
391   }
392 
393   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_DATA_INITIALIZED: sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, send_mutex=initialized", sockfd);
394 
395   // Enable TCP_NODELAY to disable Nagle's algorithm
396   // This ensures small packets are sent immediately instead of being buffered
397   int nodelay = 1;
398   if (setsockopt(sockfd, IPPROTO_TCP, TCP_NODELAY, (const char *)&nodelay, sizeof(nodelay)) < 0) {
399     log_warn("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ⚠️  TCP_NODELAY_FAILED: sockfd=%d, %s", sockfd, SAFE_STRERROR(errno));
400     // Continue anyway - this is not fatal
401   } else {
402     log_debug("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] 🟢 TCP_NODELAY_SET: sockfd=%d", sockfd);
403   }
404 
405   // Initialize transport
406   transport->methods = &tcp_methods;
407   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
408   transport->impl_data = tcp_data;
409 
410   // Register transport and impl_data before logging so named replacements work
411   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
412   NAMED_REGISTER(tcp_data, "impl", "tcp_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
413 
414   log_info("[TCP_CREATE_STATE] ✅ CREATE_COMPLETE: transport=%p, sockfd=%d, is_connected=true, crypto=%s",
415            (void *)transport, sockfd, crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
416 
417   return transport;
418 }






References acip_transport::crypto_ctx, errno,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data, INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE,
    tcp_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, log_warn,
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER,
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SAFE_STRERROR, tcp_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    SET_ERRNO, and tcp_transport_data_t::sockfd.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(), add_client(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), server_connection_establish(), and
    server_main().






void
  acip_transport_destroy (acip_transport_t * transport)


Destroy transport and free all resources.


Parameters


transport Transport to destroy (may be NULL)


Note


Calls close() first if still connected



Frees the transport structure itself




Safe to pass NULL




Definition at line 424 of file tcp/transport.c.


424                                                          {
425   if (!transport) {
426     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⚠️  NULL_TRANSPORT: nothing to destroy");
427     return;
428   }
429 
430   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 DESTROY_START: transport=%p, impl_data=%p", (void *)transport, transport->impl_data);
431 
432   // Get type before we destroy for logging
433   acip_transport_type_t type = 0;
434   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->get_type) {
435     type = transport->methods->get_type(transport);
436     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 📋 TRANSPORT_TYPE: type=%d (1=TCP, 2=WebSocket, 3=WebRTC, ...)", type);
437   }
438 
439   // Close if still connected
440   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->close && transport->methods->is_connected &&
441       transport->methods->is_connected(transport)) {
442     log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 STILL_CONNECTED: calling close() first (type=%d)", type);
443     asciichat_error_t close_result = transport->methods->close(transport);
444     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔴 CLOSE_CALLED: result=%d", close_result != ASCIICHAT_OK ? -1 : 0);
445   } else {
446     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ ALREADY_CLOSED: skipping close (type=%d)", type);
447   }
448 
449   // Call custom destroy implementation if provided
450   if (transport->methods && transport->methods->destroy_impl) {
451     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🔧 CALLING_DESTROY_IMPL: type=%d", type);
452     transport->methods->destroy_impl(transport);
453     log_info("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_IMPL_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
454   } else {
455     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ⏭️  NO_CUSTOM_DESTROY: type=%d (using generic cleanup)", type);
456   }
457 
458   // Free implementation data
459   if (transport->impl_data) {
460     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_IMPL_DATA: %p", transport->impl_data);
461     NAMED_UNREGISTER(transport->impl_data);
462     SAFE_FREE(transport->impl_data);
463     log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ IMPL_DATA_FREED");
464   }
465 
466   // Free transport structure
467   log_debug("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] 🗑️  FREEING_TRANSPORT: %p", (void *)transport);
468   SAFE_FREE(transport);
469 
470   log_warn("[TRANSPORT_DESTROY] ✅ DESTROY_COMPLETE: type=%d", type);
471 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK,
    acip_transport_methods_t::close,
    acip_transport_methods_t::destroy_impl,
    acip_transport_methods_t::get_type, acip_transport::impl_data,
    acip_transport_methods_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, log_warn, acip_transport::methods,
    NAMED_UNREGISTER, and SAFE_FREE.


Referenced by acds_client_handler(),
    connection_attempt_tcp(), connection_context_cleanup(),
    discovery_session_destroy(), remove_client(),
    server_connection_close(), server_connection_establish(),
    server_connection_set_transport(), and server_main().






acip_transport_t * acip_webrtc_transport_create (struct
  webrtc_peer_connection * peer_conn, struct webrtc_data_channel *
  data_channel, crypto_context_t * crypto_ctx)


Definition at line 368 of file
  webrtc/transport.c.


369                                                                              {
370   if (!peer_conn || !data_channel) {
371     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "peer_conn and data_channel are required");
372     return NULL;
373   }
374 
375   // Allocate transport structure
376   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
377   if (!transport) {
378     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport");
379     return NULL;
380   }
381 
382   // Allocate WebRTC-specific data
383   webrtc_transport_data_t *wrtc_data = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(webrtc_transport_data_t), webrtc_transport_data_t *);
384   if (!wrtc_data) {
385     SAFE_FREE(transport);
386     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebRTC transport data");
387     return NULL;
388   }
389 
390   // Create receive queue
391   wrtc_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(webrtc_recv_msg_t), WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
392   if (!wrtc_data->recv_queue) {
393     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
394     SAFE_FREE(transport);
395     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
396     return NULL;
397   }
398 
399   // Initialize synchronization primitives
400   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex, "webrtc_queue") != 0) {
401     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
402     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
403     SAFE_FREE(transport);
404     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue mutex");
405     return NULL;
406   }
407 
408   if (cond_init(&wrtc_data->queue_cond, "queue") != 0) {
409     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
410     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
411     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
412     SAFE_FREE(transport);
413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize queue condition variable");
414     return NULL;
415   }
416 
417   if (mutex_init(&wrtc_data->state_mutex, "webrtc_state") != 0) {
418     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
419     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
420     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
421     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
422     SAFE_FREE(transport);
423     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
424     return NULL;
425   }
426 
427   // Initialize WebRTC data
428   wrtc_data->peer_conn = peer_conn;
429   wrtc_data->data_channel = data_channel;
430   wrtc_data->is_connected = false; // Will be set to true in on_open callback
431 
432   // Register DataChannel callbacks
433   webrtc_datachannel_callbacks_t callbacks = {
434       .on_open = webrtc_on_open,
435       .on_close = webrtc_on_close,
436       .on_error = webrtc_on_error,
437       .on_message = webrtc_on_message,
438       .user_data = wrtc_data,
439   };
440 
441   asciichat_error_t result = webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(data_channel, &callbacks);
442   if (result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
443     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
444     cond_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_cond);
445     mutex_destroy(&wrtc_data->queue_mutex);
446     ringbuffer_destroy(wrtc_data->recv_queue);
447     SAFE_FREE(wrtc_data);
448     SAFE_FREE(transport);
449     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to set DataChannel callbacks");
450     return NULL;
451   }
452 
453   // IMPORTANT: The transport is always created from peer_manager's on_datachannel_open callback,
454   // which means the DataChannel is ALREADY OPEN when we get here. However, by setting our own
455   // callbacks above (webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks), we replaced the callbacks that would
456   // have set dc->is_open=true. So we need to manually mark both the transport AND the DataChannel
457   // as open/connected now.
458   //
459   // We cannot rely on webrtc_on_open being called later because:
460   // 1. The DataChannel is already open
461   // 2. libdatachannel won't fire the open event again
462   // 3. Setting callbacks after open doesn't trigger a retroactive open event
463 
464   // Mark DataChannel as open (needed for webrtc_datachannel_send() check)
465   webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(data_channel, true);
466 
467   // Mark transport as connected
468   mutex_lock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
469   wrtc_data->is_connected = true;
470   mutex_unlock(&wrtc_data->state_mutex);
471   log_debug("Transport and DataChannel marked as connected/open (already open from peer_manager callback)");
472 
473   // Initialize transport
474   transport->methods = &webrtc_methods;
475   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
476   transport->impl_data = wrtc_data;
477 
478   log_info("Created WebRTC transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
479 
480   // Register WebRTC transport implementation data with transport as parent
481   NAMED_REGISTER(wrtc_data, "impl", "webrtc_impl", "0x%tx", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
482 
483   return transport;
484 }






References ASCIICHAT_OK, cond_destroy(),
    cond_init(), acip_transport::crypto_ctx,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::data_channel, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_MEMORY,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), mutex_lock, mutex_unlock,
    NAMED_REGISTER, webrtc_transport_data_t::peer_conn,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_cond,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::queue_mutex,
    webrtc_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_FREE, SAFE_MALLOC,
    SET_ERRNO, webrtc_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    webrtc_datachannel_set_callbacks(),
    webrtc_datachannel_set_open_state(), and
    WEBRTC_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE.






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_client_transport_create
  (const char * name, const char * url, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket client transport. Connects to a WebSocket server
    at the specified URL and creates a transport for sending/receiving ACIP
    packets over the WebSocket connection.




Parameters


name Debug name for tracking this transport
  (required, e.g., "transport_websocket_client")


url WebSocket URL (e.g., "ws://localhost:27225" or
  "wss://example.com/path")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Note


The URL must include the protocol (ws:// or wss://)



Port defaults to 80 for ws:// and 443 for wss:// if not
  specified




Connection is established synchronously during creation




Parameters


url WebSocket URL (e.g.,
  "ws://localhost:27225")


crypto_ctx Optional encryption context (can be NULL)


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on failure


Definition at line 1397 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1398                                                                                        {
1399   if (!name) {
1400     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1401     return NULL;
1402   }
1403 
1404   if (!url) {
1405     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "url is required");
1406     return NULL;
1407   }
1408 
1409   // Parse URL to extract host, port, and path
1410   // Format: ws://host:port/path or wss://host:port/path
1411   const char *protocol_end = strstr(url, "://");
1412   if (!protocol_end) {
1413     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid WebSocket URL format (missing ://)");
1414     return NULL;
1415   }
1416 
1417   bool use_ssl = (strncmp(url, "wss://", 6) == 0);
1418   const char *host_start = protocol_end + 3;
1419 
1420   // Find port (if specified)
1421   const char *port_start = strchr(host_start, ':');
1422   const char *path_start = strchr(host_start, '/');
1423 
1424   char host[256] = {0};
1425   int port = use_ssl ? 443 : 27226; // Default: wss:// uses 443, ws:// uses 27226 (ascii-chat WebSocket port)
1426   char path[256] = "/";
1427 
1428   if (port_start && (!path_start || port_start < path_start)) {
1429     // Port is specified
1430     size_t host_len = port_start - host_start;
1431     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1432       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1433       return NULL;
1434     }
1435     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1436     host[host_len] = '\0';
1437 
1438     // Extract port
1439     char *endptr;
1440     errno = 0;
1441     long port_val = strtol(port_start + 1, &endptr, 10);
1442     if (*endptr != '\0' || errno != 0 || port_val <= 0 || port_val > 65535) {
1443       log_error("websocket_transport_create: Invalid port number: %s", port_start + 1);
1444       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid port number");
1445       return NULL;
1446     }
1447     port = (uint16_t)port_val;
1448   } else {
1449     // No port specified, use default
1450     size_t host_len = path_start ? (size_t)(path_start - host_start) : strlen(host_start);
1451     if (host_len >= sizeof(host)) {
1452       SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Host name too long");
1453       return NULL;
1454     }
1455     memcpy(host, host_start, host_len);
1456     host[host_len] = '\0';
1457   }
1458 
1459   // Extract path
1460   if (path_start) {
1461     SAFE_STRNCPY(path, path_start, sizeof(path) - 1);
1462     path[sizeof(path) - 1] = '\0';
1463   }
1464 
1465   log_info("Connecting to WebSocket: %s (host=%s, port=%d, path=%s, ssl=%d)", url, host, port, path, use_ssl);
1466 
1467   // Allocate transport structure
1468   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1469   if (!transport) {
1470     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1471     return NULL;
1472   }
1473 
1474   // Allocate WebSocket-specific data
1475   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1476       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1477   if (!ws_data) {
1478     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1479     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1480     return NULL;
1481   }
1482 
1483   // Initialize destruction state flag (CALLOC zero-initializes, but explicit for clarity)
1484   atomic_store_bool(&ws_data->is_destroying, false);
1485 
1486   // Create receive queue
1487   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1488   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1489     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1490     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1491     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1492     return NULL;
1493   }
1494 
1495   // Create send queue for client transport to buffer outgoing messages
1496   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1497   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1498     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1499     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1500     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1501     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1502     return NULL;
1503   }
1504 
1505   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1506   char recv_name[64], state_name[64];
1507   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1508   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1509 
1510   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1511     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1512     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1513     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1514     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1515     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1516     return NULL;
1517   }
1518 
1519   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1520     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1521     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1522     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1523     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1524     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1525     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1526     return NULL;
1527   }
1528 
1529   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1530     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1531     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1532     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1533     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1534     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1535     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1536     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1537     return NULL;
1538   }
1539 
1540   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1541     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1542     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1543     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1544     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1545     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1546     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1547     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1548     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1549     return NULL;
1550   }
1551 
1552   // Allocate initial send buffer
1553   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = LWS_PRE + 524288; // Match tx_packet_size (512KB)
1554   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(ws_data->send_buffer_capacity, uint8_t *);
1555   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1556     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1557     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1558     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1559     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1560     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1561     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1562     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1563     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1564     return NULL;
1565   }
1566 
1567   // Create libwebsockets context
1568   // Protocol array must persist for lifetime of context - use static
1569   // Register "http" protocol for WebSocket connections - matches server-side protocol name
1570   // This ensures libwebsockets will invoke our websocket_callback during upgrade handshake and communication
1571   static struct lws_protocols client_protocols[] = {
1572       {
1573           "http", // Default HTTP protocol - handles WebSocket upgrade and ACIP communication
1574           websocket_callback,
1575           0,      // Per-session data - using connect_info.userdata instead
1576           524288, // RX buffer size
1577           0,      // ID
1578           NULL,   // User pointer (set from connect_info.userdata)
1579           524288  // TX packet size
1580       },
1581       {NULL, NULL, 0, 0, 0, NULL, 0} // Terminator
1582   };
1583 
1584   // Disable client compression for now - causes assertion in lws_set_extension_option()
1585   // This is a known issue with libwebsockets permessage-deflate negotiation
1586   // Server-side compression is still enabled, data will be compressed from server to client
1587   // but client->server traffic remains uncompressed (acceptable since client sends less data)
1588   // static const struct lws_extension client_extensions[] = {
1589   //     {"permessage-deflate", lws_extension_callback_pm_deflate, "permessage-deflate; client_max_window_bits=15"},
1590   //     {NULL, NULL, NULL}};
1591 
1592   struct lws_context_creation_info info;
1593   memset(&info, 0, sizeof(info));
1594   info.port = CONTEXT_PORT_NO_LISTEN; // Client mode - no listening
1595   info.protocols = client_protocols;
1596   info.gid = (gid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1597   info.uid = (uid_t)-1; // Cast to avoid undefined behavior with unsigned type
1598   // Initialize SSL globally for client (required even for clients to use OpenSSL)
1599   // The LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT flag works for both servers and clients
1600   info.options = LWS_SERVER_OPTION_DO_SSL_GLOBAL_INIT;
1601   info.extensions = NULL; // Disable client compression due to lws_set_extension_option() assertion
1602 
1603   // Increase per-thread service buffer to prevent fragmentation of large messages
1604   // Default is 4KB, causing 291KB frames to fragment into 73 × 4KB chunks
1605   // Increase to 512KB to match server and allow larger WebSocket frames without fragmentation
1606   info.pt_serv_buf_size = 512 * 1024; // 512KB per-thread service buffer
1607 
1608   // Configure keep-alive to prevent idle disconnects during handshake
1609   // PING every 30 seconds if idle, close after 35 seconds total with no response
1610   static const lws_retry_bo_t client_keep_alive_policy = {
1611       .secs_since_valid_ping = 30,   // Send PING after 30s idle
1612       .secs_since_valid_hangup = 35, // Hangup if still idle after 35s
1613   };
1614   info.retry_and_idle_policy = &client_keep_alive_policy;
1615 
1616   // Enable libwebsockets logging through centralized logging system
1617   lws_log_init_client();
1618 
1619   ws_data->context = lws_create_context(&info);
1620 
1621   if (!ws_data->context) {
1622     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1623     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1624     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1625     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1626     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1627     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1628     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1629     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1630     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1631     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to create libwebsockets context");
1632     return NULL;
1633   }
1634 
1635   // Initialize connection state flags BEFORE calling lws_client_connect_via_info
1636   // The callback might fire immediately, so all fields must be ready
1637   ws_data->is_connected = false;      // Will be set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_ESTABLISHED
1638   ws_data->connection_failed = false; // Set to true in LWS_CALLBACK_CLIENT_CONNECTION_ERROR
1639   ws_data->owns_context = true;       // Client transport owns the context
1640 
1641   // Connect to WebSocket server
1642   log_debug("Initiating WebSocket connection to %s:%d%s", host, port, path);
1643   struct lws_client_connect_info connect_info;
1644   memset(&connect_info, 0, sizeof(connect_info));
1645   connect_info.context = ws_data->context;
1646   connect_info.address = host;
1647   connect_info.port = port;
1648   connect_info.path = path;
1649   connect_info.host = host;
1650   connect_info.origin = host;
1651   // Request the ACIP subprotocol so server routes to acip_protocol handler
1652   // This ensures WebSocket upgrade includes Sec-WebSocket-Protocol: acip
1653   connect_info.local_protocol_name = "acip";
1654   connect_info.protocol = "acip";
1655   // Use SSL + skip server certificate hostname verification + allow self-signed certs (for development)
1656   connect_info.ssl_connection =
1657       use_ssl ? (LCCSCF_USE_SSL | LCCSCF_SKIP_SERVER_CERT_HOSTNAME_CHECK | LCCSCF_ALLOW_SELFSIGNED) : 0;
1658   connect_info.userdata = ws_data;
1659 
1660   log_debug("Calling lws_client_connect_via_info...");
1661   ws_data->wsi = lws_client_connect_via_info(&connect_info);
1662   log_debug("lws_client_connect_via_info returned: %p", (void *)ws_data->wsi);
1663   if (!ws_data->wsi) {
1664     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1665     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1666     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1667     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1668     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1669     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1670     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1671     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1672     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1673     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1674     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to connect to WebSocket server");
1675     return NULL;
1676   }
1677 
1678   // Initialize transport
1679   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1680   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1681   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1682 
1683   // Start service thread immediately - it will drive TLS handshake with frequent polling
1684   // The service thread checks is_destroying and context validity before calling lws_service,
1685   // so it's safe to start it right away without delays.
1686   ws_data->service_running = true;
1687   if (asciichat_thread_create(&ws_data->service_thread, "ws_service", websocket_service_thread, ws_data) != 0) {
1688     log_error("Failed to create WebSocket service thread");
1689     ws_data->service_running = false;
1690     lws_context_destroy(ws_data->context);
1691     SAFE_FREE(ws_data->send_buffer);
1692     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1693     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1694     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1695     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1696     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1697     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1698     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1699     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1700     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INTERNAL, "Failed to create service thread");
1701     return NULL;
1702   }
1703   log_debug("WebSocket service thread started");
1704 
1705   // CRITICAL FIX: Do NOT block the main thread waiting for connection!
1706   // This prevents stdin/keyboard input from being processed and causes the client to hang.
1707   // The service thread will establish the connection asynchronously.
1708   // The protocol layer (recv) will detect if connection fails and handle it there.
1709   // Return immediately so the main thread can respond to keyboard input.
1710   log_debug("WebSocket transport created, service thread will establish connection asynchronously");
1711 
1712   // Register transport
1713   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1714 
1715   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1716   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1717 
1718   // Return transport immediately - connection will be established by service thread
1719   // If connection fails, recv() will detect is_connected=false and return error
1720   return transport;
1721 }






References asciichat_thread_create(),
    atomic_store_bool(), cond_destroy(), cond_init(),
    websocket_transport_data_t::connection_failed,
    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, errno, ERROR_INTERNAL,
    ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY,
    ERROR_NETWORK, acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_destroying, log_debug,
    log_error, log_info, lws_log_init_client(),
    acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(), mutex_init(),
    NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT, NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, SAFE_STRNCPY,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_running,
    websocket_transport_data_t::service_thread, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.


Referenced by server_connection_establish(), and
    websocket_client_connect().






acip_transport_t * acip_websocket_server_transport_create
  (const char * name, struct lws * wsi, crypto_context_t *
  crypto_ctx)


Create WebSocket server transport from existing connection. Wraps
    an already-established libwebsockets connection (from server accept). Used
    by websocket_server module to create transports for incoming
  clients.




Parameters


wsi Established libwebsockets connection (not
  owned by transport)


crypto_ctx Optional crypto context


Returns


Transport instance or NULL on error


Definition at line 1733 of file
    websocket/transport.c.


1734                                                                                        {
1735   if (!name) {
1736     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_STATE, "Transport name is required");
1737     return NULL;
1738   }
1739 
1740   if (!wsi) {
1741     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Invalid wsi parameter");
1742     return NULL;
1743   }
1744 
1745   // Allocate transport structure
1746   acip_transport_t *transport = SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(acip_transport_t), acip_transport_t *);
1747   if (!transport) {
1748     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport");
1749     return NULL;
1750   }
1751 
1752   // Allocate transport-specific data
1753   websocket_transport_data_t *ws_data =
1754       SAFE_CALLOC(1, sizeof(websocket_transport_data_t), websocket_transport_data_t *);
1755   if (!ws_data) {
1756     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1757     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate WebSocket transport data");
1758     return NULL;
1759   }
1760 
1761   // Initialize receive queue
1762   ws_data->recv_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE);
1763   if (!ws_data->recv_queue) {
1764     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1765     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1766     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create receive queue");
1767     return NULL;
1768   }
1769 
1770   // Initialize send queue (for server-side transports)
1771   ws_data->send_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(websocket_recv_msg_t), WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE);
1772   if (!ws_data->send_queue) {
1773     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1774     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1775     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1776     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create send queue");
1777     return NULL;
1778   }
1779 
1780   // Initialize pending-free queue for deferred buffer freeing
1781   // permessage-deflate compression holds buffer references asynchronously,
1782   // so we defer freeing to prevent use-after-free errors
1783   ws_data->pending_free_queue = ringbuffer_create(sizeof(pending_free_item_t), 256);
1784   if (!ws_data->pending_free_queue) {
1785     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1786     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1787     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1788     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1789     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create pending-free queue");
1790     return NULL;
1791   }
1792 
1793   // Initialize synchronization primitives with transport-aware names
1794   char recv_name[64], send_name[64], state_name[64], pending_free_name[64];
1795   snprintf(recv_name, sizeof(recv_name), "recv_%s", name);
1796   snprintf(send_name, sizeof(send_name), "send_%s", name);
1797   snprintf(state_name, sizeof(state_name), "state_%s", name);
1798   snprintf(pending_free_name, sizeof(pending_free_name), "pending_free_%s", name);
1799 
1800   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->recv_mutex, recv_name) != 0) {
1801     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1802     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1803     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1804     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1805     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv mutex");
1806     return NULL;
1807   }
1808 
1809   if (cond_init(&ws_data->recv_cond, recv_name) != 0) {
1810     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1811     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1812     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1813     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1814     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1815     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize recv condition variable");
1816     return NULL;
1817   }
1818 
1819   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->send_mutex, send_name) != 0) {
1820     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1821     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1822     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1823     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1824     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1825     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1826     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize send mutex");
1827     return NULL;
1828   }
1829 
1830   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->state_mutex, state_name) != 0) {
1831     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1832     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1833     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1834     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1835     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1836     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1837     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1838     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state mutex");
1839     return NULL;
1840   }
1841 
1842   if (cond_init(&ws_data->state_cond, state_name) != 0) {
1843     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1844     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1845     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1846     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1847     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1848     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1849     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1850     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1851     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize state condition variable");
1852     return NULL;
1853   }
1854 
1855   if (mutex_init(&ws_data->pending_free_mutex, pending_free_name) != 0) {
1856     cond_destroy(&ws_data->state_cond);
1857     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1858     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1859     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1860     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1861     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1862     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1863     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->pending_free_queue);
1864     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1865     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1866     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize pending-free mutex");
1867     return NULL;
1868   }
1869 
1870   // Allocate send buffer with LWS_PRE padding
1871   size_t initial_capacity = 4096 + LWS_PRE;
1872   ws_data->send_buffer = SAFE_MALLOC(initial_capacity, uint8_t *);
1873   if (!ws_data->send_buffer) {
1874     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->state_mutex);
1875     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->send_mutex);
1876     cond_destroy(&ws_data->recv_cond);
1877     mutex_destroy(&ws_data->recv_mutex);
1878     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->recv_queue);
1879     ringbuffer_destroy(ws_data->send_queue);
1880     SAFE_FREE(ws_data);
1881     SAFE_FREE(transport);
1882     SET_ERRNO(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to allocate send buffer");
1883     return NULL;
1884   }
1885   ws_data->send_buffer_capacity = initial_capacity;
1886 
1887   // Store connection info (server-side: no context ownership, connection already established)
1888   ws_data->wsi = wsi;
1889   ws_data->context = lws_get_context(wsi); // Get context from wsi (not owned)
1890   ws_data->owns_context = false;           // Server owns context, not transport
1891   ws_data->is_connected = true;            // Already connected (server-side)
1892   log_info("[WEBSOCKET_TRANSPORT_CREATE] ★★★ SERVER TRANSPORT CREATED: is_connected=true, wsi=%p, ws_data=%p",
1893            (void *)wsi, (void *)ws_data);
1894   log_debug("Server transport created: is_connected=true, wsi=%p", (void *)wsi);
1895 
1896   // Initialize transport
1897   transport->methods = &websocket_methods;
1898   transport->crypto_ctx = crypto_ctx;
1899   transport->impl_data = ws_data;
1900 
1901   log_info("Created WebSocket server transport (crypto: %s)", crypto_ctx ? "enabled" : "disabled");
1902 
1903   // Register transport
1904   NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT(transport, name, NULL);
1905 
1906   // Register websocket implementation data with transport as parent
1907   (void)NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL(ws_data, "impl", (uintptr_t)(const void *)(transport));
1908 
1909   return transport;
1910 }

    websocket_transport_data_t::context,
    acip_transport::crypto_ctx, ERROR_INVALID_PARAM,
    ERROR_INVALID_STATE, ERROR_MEMORY, ERROR_NETWORK,
    acip_transport::impl_data,
    websocket_transport_data_t::is_connected, log_debug,
    log_info, acip_transport::methods, mutex_destroy(),
    mutex_init(), NAMED_REGISTER_TRANSPORT,
    NAMED_REGISTER_WEBSOCKET_IMPL,
    websocket_transport_data_t::owns_context,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::pending_free_queue,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::recv_queue, ringbuffer_create(),
    ringbuffer_destroy(), SAFE_CALLOC, SAFE_FREE,
    SAFE_MALLOC, websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_buffer_capacity,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_mutex,
    websocket_transport_data_t::send_queue, SET_ERRNO,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_cond,
    websocket_transport_data_t::state_mutex,
    WEBSOCKET_RECV_QUEUE_SIZE, WEBSOCKET_SEND_QUEUE_SIZE, and
    websocket_transport_data_t::wsi.








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.19
    ascii-chat
